plan: Analyze the sales data for this collectible, filtering for the most recent three months of activity to establish current market value. Since all data points are NM condition, I will establish a price range for NM only. I will weight recent sold/auction data more heavily than unsold listings, and apply the pre-set grade chart interpretation for the final range.
reasoning_steps: Step 1: Identify the most recent date in the dataset — 2025-12-20 — and define 'recent three months' as approximately October–December 2025.
Step 2: Filter for records within that window: IDs 14 (2025-11-08), 15 (2025-12-01), and 16 (2025-12-20).

Step 3: Note listing types — IDs 14 and 16 are 'unsold' (asking prices), ID 15 is an 'auction' (actual transaction). The auction result of $220.29 for a sealed 50th pressing is the strongest market signal.

Step 4: Unsold listings ($190.01 and $180.98) represent floor-level asking prices that did not clear — they suggest sellers believe value is above ~$180, but buyers were not convinced at those prices.

Step 5: Cross-reference with older sold data — there are no confirmed 'sold' listings in the dataset outside of the one auction, making price discovery reliant on asking prices and one auction outlier.

Step 6: Apply the pre-set grade chart: NM range is $185–$225. This aligns with the auction high ($220.29) and sits above the unsold floor prices, which is consistent with unsold listings being slightly underpriced or over-conditioned.
Step 7: All records are NM condition — no other grades are present in the data, so only NM is reported.
evidence: The only confirmed transaction in the recent three-month window was a sealed 50th anniversary pressing that sold at auction for $220.29 (Dec 2025), anchoring the upper bound of the NM range.
Two unsold Discogs listings from Nov–Dec 2025 priced at $190.01 and $180.98 suggest sellers view $180–$190 as a reasonable floor, supporting the $185 low estimate.
Historical NM asking prices on Discogs from 2023–2024 clustered between $169–$195, indicating the current $185–$225 range reflects modest upward price movement over time.
assumptions: Unsold listings are treated as indicative of seller sentiment and asking-price floors, not confirmed market value.
The sealed 50th anniversary pressing auction result ($220.29) is assumed to represent a premium variant; standard NM copies likely trade closer to the $185–$200 range.
All NM copies are treated as functionally equivalent for grading purposes, despite meaningful variation in pressings, inserts, and edition type across listings.
limitations: Only one actual sold/completed transaction exists in the entire dataset — the vast majority of data points are unsold asking prices, which significantly limits pricing accuracy.
No sold comparables exist for standard (non-sealed, non-special) NM copies in the recent three-month window, making the low end of the range an estimate based on older data.
Amazon listings in the dataset (all unsold, 2022–2023) may reflect retail or new-product pricing rather than secondary market collector value, and could distort baseline assumptions.
alternative_interpretations: The $220.29 auction result may be an outlier driven by auction dynamics or the 'sealed' premium rather than true NM market value — standard open NM copies may realistically top out closer to $195.

The pattern of persistent unsold listings across multiple platforms could indicate the market is softer than asking prices suggest, and actual clearing prices may fall below $185 for common pressings.

Price variation across descriptions (with vs. without inserts, US vs. UK pressings, reissues vs. originals) suggests this collectible may have meaningful sub-grade tiers within NM that a single range cannot fully capture.
